_note: SYNTHETIC DEMO FIXTURE — not investment advice, not real CMG disclosures. Numbers illustrative; the sell-side note is fictional. Pre-call structured input for the Diligence-Call Copilot demo: the fund's question list + thesis + modeled assumptions the copilot grounds follow-ups and contradiction flags against.
thesis: CMG is a high-return unit-growth compounder: Chipotlanes and throughput push new-unit economics toward a ~7,000-restaurant North American runway, restaurant-level margins hold in the mid-to-high 20s through food and labor inflation, and digital plus loyalty deepen the moat.
thesis_memo: We are long CMG on a long, high-return unit-growth runway toward ~7,000 NA restaurants at improving new-unit economics (Chipotlane mix). Two load-bearing assumptions we need the sell-side analyst to defend on this call: (1) comp is TRAFFIC-led, with carried pricing only a few points — price-reliant comp is lower quality and signals less runway; and (2) restaurant-level margins hold in the mid-to-high 20s as beef and wage inflation are offset by pricing and throughput. If either cracks, the premium multiple is harder to underwrite.

modeled_assumptions.unit_growth: 8-10% net new units/yr toward a ~7,000 NA target
modeled_assumptions.comp_price_contribution: ~3-4 pts of comp from menu pricing carried over the trailing year (traffic-led)

pillars.thesis: Downside is bounded by value perception

questions.text: You've guided to ~7,000 North American restaurants long term — what's the current new-unit pace, and are Chipotlanes still the majority of openings with better returns?
questions.why: Unit growth is the spine of the thesis. If the pace slips or Chipotlane returns fade, the compounding story weakens.
questions.demo_role: clean tick — answered early and cleanly

questions.text: How much of recent comp growth is transactions versus the menu pricing you've carried over the past year?
questions.why: Comp QUALITY is the differentiator. The note frames the comp as traffic-led; if it's actually price-led, the runway and premium multiple are weaker than underwritten.
questions.demo_role: HERO — researcher over-claims 'all traffic' -> partial/amber + contradiction vs note -> copilot fires grounded follow-up -> closes green with the real split

questions.text: What throughput initiatives are live, and are they actually lifting peak-hour transactions?
questions.why: Throughput is the underrated comp lever and a margin tailwind through fixed-cost leverage at peak.

questions.text: Where are restaurant-level margins running, and can they hold against beef and wage inflation?
questions.why: Margin durability underpins the cash generation that self-funds the unit growth.

questions.text: How durable is the digital mix, and how are you monetizing the loyalty base?
questions.why: Digital and loyalty drive frequency — the cleaner (traffic) lever for comp — and a higher-margin channel.

questions.text: What's the single biggest risk — if the consumer pulls back, does value perception hold your traffic?
questions.why: Downside framing. A consumer pullback is the obvious bear case; value perception is the proposed cushion.
questions.demo_role: late grey -> green — stays unanswered until the copilot nudges near the end, then closes
